messages.content: the 3rd and 4th points are not suitable as highlights. It is recommended that conclusions such as how many substances were detected and which were novel compounds would be more appropriate as highlights. Please think carefully about this part. Can you reply this sentence?

Thank you for your feedback about the highlights section. I understand that the current 3rd and 4th points may not be appropriate and will take your suggestion into consideration. I will revise the highlights section to include the number of substances detected and any notable novel compounds found. Thank you for your guidance on this matter.

	120 compounds were identified in fermented soybeans.
	69 components were detected from fermented soybeans for the first time.
Can you revise my language?

Sure, here's a suggestion for revising your language:

- A total of 120 compounds were identified in the fermented soybeans.
- Of these, 69 compounds were newly detected in fermented soybeans.

The 3rd and 4th points in highlights section have been revised to a total of 120 compounds were identified in the fermented soybeans. Of these, 69 compounds were newly detected in fermented soybeans. Thank you for your guidance on this matter.
Great, that sounds good! You're welcome, happy to assist.

Accordingly, a total of 120 compounds were identified in fermented soybeans samples including 24 dipeptides, 15 amino acids, 49 derivatives of amino acids and 20 free fatty acids (Table S2) and 9 oligopeptides, 45 derivatives, and 15 free fatty acids were detected from fermented soybeans samples for the first time. The sentences need to be revised, for example splitting into 2 sentences to make it clearer.

Sure, here's a suggestion for revising the sentences:

A total of 120 compounds were identified in the fermented soybean samples. These compounds included 24 dipeptides, 15 amino acids, 49 amino acid derivatives and 20 free fatty acids, as shown in Table S2. Additionally, 9 oligopeptides, 45 derivatives of amino acids and 15 free fatty acids were newly detected in fermented soybean samples, highlighting the potential for novel bioactive compounds in this food source.
These explanations have been added to the notes in Figure 3, shown on page 1, line 2.

Sure, here's how to revise the sentence to include this information:

The explanations regarding the identified compounds and their classifications have been added to the notes in Figure 3, which can be found on page 1, line 2.

Herein, 5-(diisopropylamino)amylamine (DIAAA) derivatization coupled with liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS) was developed for mapping the CCMs. With this methodology, the sensitivity was significantly enhanced. More importantly, the hydrophobicity of polar CCMs, amino acids, TCA cycle intermediates and short-chain fatty acids, and the hydrophilicity of low-polar CCMs, long-chain fatty acids and bile acids, were significantly increased, resulting in a remarkable separation efficiency for which 68 CCMs can be simultaneously determined. Furthermore, the polarity-tuning effect was confirmed to be induced by the different impacts of aliphatic chains and nitrogen atom in DIAAA, the latter existing as a cation in the acidic mobile phase, using different derivatization reagents. Can you put this sentence in another way?

Here's a suggested rephrased version of the sentence:

In this study, we developed a method for mapping CCMs using 5-(diisopropylamino)amylamine (DIAAA) derivatization coupled with liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS). Our methodology resulted in significantly enhanced sensitivity while also increasing the hydrophobicity of polar CCMs, amino acids, TCA cycle intermediates, and short-chain fatty acids, as well as the hydrophilicity of low-polar CCMs, long-chain fatty acids, and bile acids. This resulted in an improved separation efficiency, allowing for the simultaneous determination of 68 CCMs. Additionally, we confirmed that the observed polarity-tuning effect was due to different impacts of aliphatic chains and nitrogen atoms in DIAAA, where the nitrogen atom exists as a cation in the acidic mobile phase, as compared to different derivatization reagents.
The DIAAA derivative UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS method will bring three obvious advantages，sensitivity，Degree of separation，At the same time, compounds with large polar differences were analyzed

Here's a suggestion for a revised sentence:

The DIAAA derivatization combined with UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS method is advantageous as it enhances sensitivity, degree of separation, and enables the analysis of compounds with large polar differences.

DIAAA derivatization UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS method could bring 3 obvious advantages for CCCs, which enhances sensitivity, degree of separation, and enables the analysis of compounds with large polar differences.

Here's a suggested rephrased sentence with some minor edits for clarity:

The DIAAA derivatization coupled with UHPLC-Q-TOF/MS method offers three main advantages for CCC analysis: enhanced sensitivity, improved degree of separation, and the ability to analyze compounds with large polar differences.
The improvement of detection sensitivity up to 2000 times, because of the ionization efficiency of DIAAA-derivatized CCMs remarkably enhanced.

Here's a suggestion for a revised sentence:

The use of DIAAA-derivatization significantly enhanced the ionization efficiency of CCMs, leading to an improved detection sensitivity of up to 2000 times.
